# Haystack

```
author

Daniel Lindsley

date

2013/07/28
```

Haystack provides modular search for Django. It features a unified, familiar
API that allows you to plug in different search backends (such as [Solr],
[Elasticsearch], [Whoosh], [Xapian], etc.) without having to modify your code.

Haystack is BSD licensed, plays nicely with third-party app without needing to
modify the source and supports advanced features like faceting, More Like This,
highlighting, spatial search and spelling suggestions.

You can find more information at <http://haystacksearch.org/>.

## Getting Help

There is a mailing list (<http://groups.google.com/group/django-haystack/>)
available for general discussion and an IRC channel (#haystack on
irc.freenode.net).

## Documentation

- Development version: <http://docs.haystacksearch.org/>
- v3.3.0: <https://django-haystack.readthedocs.io/en/v3.3.0/>
- v2.8.1: <https://django-haystack.readthedocs.io/en/v2.8.1/>

See the [changelog](docs/changelog.rst)

## Requirements

Haystack has a relatively easily-met set of requirements.

- Python 3.8+
- Django 3-5

Additionally, each backend has its own requirements. You should refer to
<https://django-haystack.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installing_search_engines.html> for more
details.

[elasticsearch]: https://www.elastic.co/products/elasticsearch
[solr]: http://lucene.apache.org/solr/
[whoosh]: https://github.com/mchaput/whoosh/
[xapian]: http://xapian.org/

classes.name: example_project.regular_app.search_indexes.DogIndex
documentation: More typical usage involves creating a subclassed `SearchIndex`. This will
provide more control over how data is indexed, generally resulting in better
search.

classes.name: example_project.bare_bones_app.search_indexes.CatIndex
documentation: For the most basic usage, you can use a subclass of
`haystack.indexes.BasicSearchIndex`, whose only requirement will be that
you create a `search/indexes/bare_bones_app/cat_text.txt` data template
for indexing.

methods.name: haystack.fields.SearchField.prepare_template
documentation:
Flattens an object for indexing.

This loads a template
(`search/indexes/{app_label}/{model_name}_{field_name}.txt`) and
returns the result of rendering that template. `object` will be in
its context.

methods.name: haystack.fields.SearchField.convert
documentation:
Handles conversion between the data found and the type of the field.

Extending classes should override this method and provide correct
data coercion.
The base implementation of a search field.
All the SearchFields variants.

classes.name: haystack.fields.FacetField
documentation:
`FacetField` is slightly different than the other fields because it can
work in conjunction with other fields as its data source.

Accepts an optional `facet_for` kwarg, which should be the field name
(not `index_fieldname`) of the field it should pull data from.

functions.name: haystack.templatetags.highlight.highlight
documentation:
Takes a block of text and highlights words from a provided query within that
block of text. Optionally accepts arguments to provide the HTML tag to wrap
highlighted word in, a CSS class to use with the tag and a maximum length of
the blurb in characters.

Syntax:

```
{% highlight <text_block> with <query> [css_class "class_name"] [html_tag "span"] [max_length 200] %}
```

Example:

```
# Highlight summary with default behavior.
{% highlight result.summary with request.query %}

# Highlight summary but wrap highlighted words with a div and the
# following CSS class.
{% highlight result.summary with request.query html_tag "div" css_class "highlight_me_please" %}

# Highlight summary but only show 40 characters.
{% highlight result.summary with request.query max_length 40 %}
```

functions.name: haystack.templatetags.more_like_this.more_like_this
documentation:
Fetches similar items from the search index to find content that is similar
to the provided model's content.

Syntax:

```
{% more_like_this model_instance as varname [for app_label.model_name,app_label.model_name,...] [limit n] %}
```

Example:

```
# Pull a full SearchQuerySet (lazy loaded) of similar content.
{% more_like_this entry as related_content %}

# Pull just the top 5 similar pieces of content.
{% more_like_this entry as related_content limit 5  %}

# Pull just the top 5 similar entries or comments.
{% more_like_this entry as related_content for "blog.entry,comments.comment" limit 5  %}
```

classes.name: haystack.constants.Indexable
documentation: A marker class in the hierarchy to indicate that it handles search data.

classes.name: haystack.exceptions.HaystackError
documentation: A generic exception for all others to extend.

documentation: Raised when a model instance has not been provided for More Like This.

documentation: Raised when incorrect arguments have been provided for faceting.

classes.name: haystack.exceptions.SpatialError
documentation: Raised when incorrect arguments have been provided for spatial.

classes.name: haystack.exceptions.SkipDocument
documentation: Raised when a document should be skipped while updating

functions.name: haystack.views.basic_search
documentation:
A more traditional view that also demonstrate an alternative
way to use Haystack.

Useful as an example of for basing heavily custom views off of.

Also has the benefit of thread-safety, which the `SearchView` class may
not be.

Template:: `search/search.html`
Context:

```
* form
  An instance of the ``form_class``. (default: ``ModelSearchForm``)
* page
  The current page of search results.
* paginator
  A paginator instance for the results.
* query
  The query received by the form.
```

methods.name: haystack.views.SearchView.__call__
documentation:
Generates the actual response to the search.

Relies on internal, overridable methods to construct the response.

methods.name: haystack.views.SearchView.build_form
documentation: Instantiates the form the class should use to process the search query.

methods.name: haystack.views.SearchView.get_query
documentation:
Returns the query provided by the user.

Returns an empty string if the query is invalid.

methods.name: haystack.views.SearchView.get_results
documentation:
Fetches the results via the form.

Returns an empty list if there's no query to search with.

methods.name: haystack.views.SearchView.build_page
documentation:
Paginates the results appropriately.

In case someone does not want to use Django's built-in pagination, it
should be a simple matter to override this method to do what they would
like.

methods.name: haystack.views.SearchView.extra_context
documentation:
Allows the addition of more context variables as needed.

Must return a dictionary.

methods.name: haystack.views.SearchView.create_response
documentation: Generates the actual HttpResponse to send back to the user.

functions.name: haystack.reset_search_queries
documentation: Per-request, reset the ghetto query log.
Probably not extraordinarily thread-safe but should only matter when
DEBUG = True.

List or tuple of models to clear.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.all
documentation: Returns all results for the query.
Methods that return a SearchQuerySet.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.none
documentation: Returns an empty result list for the query.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.filter
documentation: Narrows the search based on certain attributes and the default operator.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.exclude
documentation: Narrows the search by ensuring certain attributes are not included.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.filter_and
documentation: Narrows the search by looking for (and including) certain attributes.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.filter_or
documentation: Narrows the search by ensuring certain attributes are not included.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.order_by
documentation: Alters the order in which the results should appear.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.highlight
documentation: Adds highlighting to the results.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.models
documentation: Accepts an arbitrary number of Model classes to include in the search.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.result_class
documentation:
Allows specifying a different class to use for results.

Overrides any previous usages. If `None` is provided, Haystack will
revert back to the default `SearchResult` object.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.boost
documentation: Boosts a certain aspect of the query.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.facet
documentation: Adds faceting to a query for the provided field.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.within
documentation: Spatial: Adds a bounding box search to the query.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.dwithin
documentation: Spatial: Adds a distance-based search to the query.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.stats
documentation: Adds stats to a query for the provided field.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.stats_facet
documentation:
Adds stats facet for the given field and facet_fields represents
the faceted fields.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.distance
documentation:
Spatial: Denotes results must have distance measurements from the
provided point.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.date_facet
documentation: Adds faceting to a query for the provided field by date.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.query_facet
documentation: Adds faceting to a query for the provided field with a custom query.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.narrow
documentation: Pushes existing facet choices into the search.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.raw_search
documentation: Passes a raw query directly to the backend.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.load_all
documentation: Efficiently populates the objects in the search results.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.auto_query
documentation:
Performs a best guess constructing the search query.

This method is somewhat naive but works well enough for the simple,
common cases.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.autocomplete
documentation:
A shortcut method to perform an autocomplete search.

Must be run against fields that are either `NgramField` or
`EdgeNgramField`.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.using
documentation:
Allows switching which connection the `SearchQuerySet` uses to
search in.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.count
documentation: Returns the total number of matching results.
Methods that do not return a SearchQuerySet.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.best_match
documentation: Returns the best/top search result that matches the query.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.latest
documentation: Returns the most recent search result that matches the query.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.more_like_this
documentation: Finds similar results to the object passed in.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.facet_counts
documentation:
Returns the facet counts found by the query.

This will cause the query to execute and should generally be used when
presenting the data.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.stats_results
documentation: Returns the stats results found by the query.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.set_spelling_query
documentation:
Set the exact text to be used to generate spelling suggestions

When making complicated queries, such as the alt parser mechanism
used by Solr dismax/edismax, this provides a convenient way to set
the a simple text string which will be used to generate spelling
suggestions without including unnecessary syntax.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.spelling_suggestion
documentation:
Returns the spelling suggestion found by the query.

To work, you must set `INCLUDE_SPELLING` within your connection's
settings dictionary to `True`. Otherwise, `None` will be returned.

This will cause the query to execute and should generally be used when
presenting the data.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.values
documentation:
Returns a list of dictionaries, each containing the key/value pairs for
the result, exactly like Django's `ValuesQuerySet`.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet.values_list
documentation:
Returns a list of field values as tuples, exactly like Django's
`QuerySet.values`.

Optionally accepts a `flat=True` kwarg, which in the case of a
single field being provided, will return a flat list of that field
rather than a list of tuples.

methods.name: haystack.query.SearchQuerySet._clone
documentation: Utility methods.

Provides a way to specify search parameters and lazily load results.

Supports chaining (a la QuerySet) to narrow the search.

classes.name: haystack.query.EmptySearchQuerySet
documentation:
A stubbed SearchQuerySet that behaves as normal but always returns no
results.

classes.name: haystack.query.ValuesListSearchQuerySet
documentation:
A `SearchQuerySet` which returns a list of field values as tuples, exactly
like Django's `ValuesListQuerySet`.

classes.name: haystack.query.ValuesSearchQuerySet
documentation:
A `SearchQuerySet` which returns a list of dictionaries, each containing
the key/value pairs for the result, exactly like Django's
`ValuesQuerySet`.

classes.name: haystack.query.RelatedSearchQuerySet

methods.name: haystack.query.RelatedSearchQuerySet.load_all_queryset
documentation:
Allows for specifying a custom `QuerySet` that changes how `load_all`
will fetch records for the provided model.

This is useful for post-processing the results from the query, enabling
things like adding `select_related` or filtering certain data.
A variant of the SearchQuerySet that can handle `load_all_queryset`s.

methods.name: haystack.backends.elasticsearch5_backend.Elasticsearch5SearchBackend.clear

classes.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchBackend

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchBackend.update
documentation:
Updates the backend when given a SearchIndex and a collection of
documents.

This method MUST be implemented by each backend, as it will be highly
specific to each one.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchBackend.remove
documentation:
Removes a document/object from the backend. Can be either a model
instance or the identifier (i.e. `app_name.model_name.id`) in the
event the object no longer exists.

This method MUST be implemented by each backend, as it will be highly
specific to each one.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchBackend.clear
documentation:
Clears the backend of all documents/objects for a collection of models.

This method MUST be implemented by each backend, as it will be highly
specific to each one.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchBackend.search
documentation:
Takes a query to search on and returns dictionary.

The query should be a string that is appropriate syntax for the backend.

The returned dictionary should contain the keys 'results' and 'hits'.
The 'results' value should be an iterable of populated SearchResult
objects. The 'hits' should be an integer count of the number of matched
results the search backend found.

This method MUST be implemented by each backend, as it will be highly
specific to each one.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchBackend.prep_value
documentation:
Hook to give the backend a chance to prep an attribute value before
sending it to the search engine. By default, just force it to unicode.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchBackend.more_like_this
documentation:
Takes a model object and returns results the backend thinks are similar.

This method MUST be implemented by each backend, as it will be highly
specific to each one.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchBackend.extract_file_contents
documentation:
Hook to allow backends which support rich-content types such as PDF,
Word, etc. extraction to process the provided file object and return
the contents for indexing

Returns None if metadata cannot be extracted; otherwise returns a
dictionary containing at least two keys:

    :contents:
                Extracted full-text content, if applicable
    :metadata:
                key:value pairs of text strings

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchBackend.build_schema
documentation:
Takes a dictionary of fields and returns schema information.

This method MUST be implemented by each backend, as it will be highly
specific to each one.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchBackend.build_models_list
documentation:
Builds a list of models for searching.

The `search` method should use this and the `django_ct` field to
narrow the results (unless the user indicates not to). This helps ignore
any results that are not currently handled models and ensures
consistent caching.
Abstract search engine base class.

classes.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode

methods.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode.__init__
documentation:
Constructs a new Node. If no connector is given, the default will be
used.

Warning: You probably don't want to pass in the 'negated' parameter. It
is NOT the same as constructing a node and calling negate() on the
result.
Start compat. Django 1.6 changed how ``tree.Node`` works, so we're going
to patch back in the original implementation until time to rewrite this
presents itself.
See https://github.com/django/django/commit/d3f00bd.

methods.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode._new_instance
documentation:
This is called to create a new instance of this class when we need new
Nodes (or subclasses) in the internal code in this class. Normally, it
just shadows __init__(). However, subclasses with an __init__ signature
that is not an extension of Node.__init__ might need to implement this
method to allow a Node to create a new instance of them (if they have
any extra setting up to do).
We need this because of django.db.models.query_utils.Q. Q. __init__() is
problematic, but it is a natural Node subclass in all other respects.

methods.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode.__deepcopy__
documentation: Utility method used by copy.deepcopy().

methods.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode.__len__
documentation: The size of a node if the number of children it has.

methods.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode.__bool__
documentation: For truth value testing.

methods.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode.__contains__
documentation: Returns True is 'other' is a direct child of this instance.

methods.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode.add
documentation:
Adds a new node to the tree. If the conn_type is the same as the root's
current connector type, the node is added to the first level.
Otherwise, the whole tree is pushed down one level and a new root
connector is created, connecting the existing tree and the new node.

methods.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode.negate
documentation:
Negate the sense of the root connector. This reorganises the children
so that the current node has a single child: a negated node containing
all the previous children. This slightly odd construction makes adding
new children behave more intuitively.

Interpreting the meaning of this negate is up to client code. This
method is useful for implementing "not" arrangements.

methods.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode.start_subtree
documentation:
Sets up internal state so that new nodes are added to a subtree of the
current node. The conn_type specifies how the sub-tree is joined to the
existing children.

methods.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode.end_subtree
documentation:
Closes off the most recently unmatched start_subtree() call.

This puts the current state into a node of the parent tree and returns
the current instances state to be the parent.

methods.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode.__repr__
documentation: End compat.

methods.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode.as_query_string
documentation:
Produces a portion of the search query from the current SQ and its
children.

methods.name: haystack.backends.SearchNode.split_expression
documentation: Parses an expression and determines the field and filter type.

Manages an individual condition within a query.

Most often, this will be a lookup to ensure that a certain word or phrase
appears in the documents being indexed. However, it also supports filtering
types (such as 'lt', 'gt', 'in' and others) for more complex lookups.

This object creates a tree, with children being a list of either more
`SQ` objects or the expressions/values themselves.

classes.name: haystack.backends.SQ

Most often, this will be a lookup to ensure that a certain word or phrase
appears in the documents being indexed. However, it also supports filtering
types (such as 'lt', 'gt', 'in' and others) for more complex lookups.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery.add_model

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery.set_limits

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery.clear_limits

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery.add_boost

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery.raw_search
documentation:
Runs a raw query (no parsing) against the backend.

This method causes the SearchQuery to ignore the standard query
generating facilities, running only what was provided instead.

Note that any kwargs passed along will override anything provided
to the rest of the `SearchQuerySet`.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery.add_within

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery.add_dwithin

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery.add_distance
documentation:
Denotes that results should include distance measurements from the

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery.add_field_facet
documentation: Adds a regular facet on a field.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery.add_date_facet
documentation: Adds a date-based facet on a field.

documentation: Adds a query facet on a field.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery.add_narrow_query
documentation:
Narrows a search to a subset of all documents per the query.

Generally used in conjunction with faceting.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery.set_result_class
documentation:
Sets the result class to use for results.

Overrides any previous usages. If `None` is provided, Haystack will
revert back to the default `SearchResult` object.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery.using
documentation:
Allows for overriding which connection should be used. This
disables the use of routers when performing the query.

If `None` is provided, it has no effect on what backend is used.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseSearchQuery._reset
documentation:
Resets the instance's internal state to appear as though no query has
been run before. Only need to tweak a few variables we check.

A base class for handling the query itself.

This class acts as an intermediary between the `SearchQuerySet` and the
`SearchBackend` itself.

The `SearchQuery` object maintains a tree of `SQ` objects. Each `SQ`
object supports what field it looks up against, what kind of lookup (i.e.
the \_\_'s), what value it's looking for, if it's a AND/OR/NOT and tracks
any children it may have. The `SearchQuery.build_query` method starts with
the root of the tree, building part of the final query at each node until
the full final query is ready for the `SearchBackend`.

Backends should extend this class and provide implementations for
`build_query_fragment`, `clean` and `run`. See the `solr` backend for an example
implementation.

methods.name: haystack.backends.BaseEngine.reset_sessions
documentation: Reset any transient connections, file handles, etc.

classes.name: haystack.generic_views.SearchMixin

methods.name: haystack.generic_views.SearchMixin.get_form_kwargs
documentation: Returns the keyword arguments for instantiating the form.

A mixin that allows adding in Haystacks search functionality into
another view class.

This mixin exhibits similar end functionality as the base Haystack search
view, but with some important distinctions oriented around greater
compatibility with Django's built-in class based views and mixins.

Normal flow:

    self.request = request

    self.form = self.build_form()
    self.query = self.get_query()
    self.results = self.get_results()

    return self.create_response()

This mixin should:

    1. Make the form
    2. Get the queryset
    3. Return the paginated queryset

classes.name: haystack.generic_views.FacetedSearchMixin
documentation:
A mixin that allows adding in a Haystack search functionality with search

A view class for searching a Haystack managed search index with

methods.name: haystack.backends.solr_backend.SolrSearchBackend.extract_file_contents
documentation:
Extract text and metadata from a structured file (PDF, MS Word, etc.)

Uses the Solr ExtractingRequestHandler, which is based on Apache Tika.
See the Solr wiki for details:

    http://wiki.apache.org/solr/ExtractingRequestHandler

Due to the way the ExtractingRequestHandler is implemented it completely
replaces the normal Haystack indexing process with several unfortunate
restrictions: only one file per request, the extracted data is added to
the index with no ability to modify it, etc. To simplify the process and
allow for more advanced use we'll run using the extract-only mode to
return the extracted data without adding it to the index so we can then
use it within Haystack's normal templating process.

Returns None if metadata cannot be extracted; otherwise returns a
dictionary containing at least two keys:

    :contents:
                Extracted full-text content, if applicable
    :metadata:
                key:value pairs of text strings

class_variables.name: haystack.models.SearchResult.object
documentation: noqa A003

A single search result. The actual object is loaded lazily by accessing
object; until then this object only stores the model, pk, and score.

Note that iterating over SearchResults and getting the object for each
result will do O(N) database queries, which may not fit your needs for
performance.
Not a Django model, but tightly tied to them and there doesn't seem to be a
better spot in the tree.

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.get_model
documentation:
Should return the `Model` class (not an instance) that the rest of the

This method is required & you must override it to return the correct class.

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.index_queryset
documentation:
Get the default QuerySet to index when doing a full update.

Subclasses can override this method to avoid indexing certain objects.

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.read_queryset
documentation:
Get the default QuerySet for read actions.

Subclasses can override this method to work with other managers.
Useful when working with default managers that filter some objects.

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.build_queryset
documentation:
Get the default QuerySet to index when doing an index update.

Subclasses can override this method to take into account related
model modification times.

The default is to use `SearchIndex.index_queryset` and filter
based on `SearchIndex.get_updated_field`

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.prepare
documentation: Fetches and adds/alters data before indexing.

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.get_content_field
documentation: Returns the field that supplies the primary document to be indexed.

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.get_field_weights
documentation: Returns a dict of fields with weight values

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.update
documentation:
Updates the entire index.

If `using` is provided, it specifies which connection should be
used. Default relies on the routers to decide which backend should
be used.

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.update_object
documentation:
Update the index for a single object. Attached to the class's
post-save hook.

If `using` is provided, it specifies which connection should be
used. Default relies on the routers to decide which backend should
be used.

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.remove_object
documentation:
Remove an object from the index. Attached to the class's
post-delete hook.

If `using` is provided, it specifies which connection should be
used. Default relies on the routers to decide which backend should
be used.

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.clear
documentation:
Clears the entire index.

If `using` is provided, it specifies which connection should be
used. Default relies on the routers to decide which backend should
be used.

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.reindex
documentation:
Completely clear the index for this model and rebuild it.

If `using` is provided, it specifies which connection should be
used. Default relies on the routers to decide which backend should
be used.

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.get_updated_field
documentation:
Get the field name that represents the updated date for the model.

If specified, this is used by the reindex command to filter out results
from the QuerySet, enabling you to reindex only recent records. This
method should either return None (reindex everything always) or a
string of the Model's DateField/DateTimeField name.

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.should_update
documentation:
Determine if an object should be updated in the index.

It's useful to override this when an object may save frequently and
cause excessive reindexing. You should check conditions on the instance
and return False if it is not to be indexed.

By default, returns True (always reindex).

methods.name: haystack.indexes.SearchIndex.load_all_queryset
documentation:
Provides the ability to override how objects get loaded in conjunction
with `SearchQuerySet.load_all`.

This is useful for post-processing the results from the query, enabling
things like adding `select_related` or filtering certain data.

By default, returns `all()` on the model's default manager.

Base class for building indexes.

An example might look like this:

```
import datetime
from haystack import indexes
from myapp.models import Note

class NoteIndex(indexes.SearchIndex, indexes.Indexable):
    text = indexes.CharField(document=True, use_template=True)
    author = indexes.CharField(model_attr='user')
    pub_date = indexes.DateTimeField(model_attr='pub_date')

    def get_model(self):
        return Note

    def index_queryset(self, using=None):
        return self.get_model().objects.filter(pub_date__lte=datetime.datetime.now())
```

Given a Django model field, return if it should be included in the
contributed SearchFields.

documentation: Given a Django field, return the appropriate index fieldname.

methods.name: haystack.indexes.ModelSearchIndex.get_fields
documentation:
Given any explicit fields to include and fields to exclude, add
additional fields based on the associated model.

Introspects the model assigned to it and generates a `SearchIndex` based on
the fields of that model.

In addition, it adds a `text` field that is the `document=True` field and
has `use_template=True` option set, just like the `BasicSearchIndex`.

Usage of this class might result in inferior `SearchIndex` objects, which
can directly affect your search results. Use this to establish basic
functionality and move to custom `SearchIndex` objects for better control.

At this time, it does not handle related fields.

functions.name: haystack.utils.app_loading.haystack_get_app_modules
documentation: Return the Python module for each installed app

functions.name: haystack.utils.app_loading.haystack_load_apps
documentation: Return a list of app labels for all installed applications which have models

methods.name: haystack.management.commands.build_solr_schema.Command.handle
documentation: Generates a Solr schema that reflects the indexes.

functions.name: haystack.utils.default_get_identifier
documentation:
Get an unique identifier for the object or a string representing the
object.

If not overridden, uses <app_label>.<object_name>.<pk>.

functions.name: haystack.utils._lookup_identifier_method
documentation:
If the user has set HAYSTACK_IDENTIFIER_METHOD, import it and return the method uncalled.
If HAYSTACK_IDENTIFIER_METHOD is not defined, return haystack.utils.default_get_identifier.

This always runs at module import time.  We keep the code in a function
so that it can be called from unit tests, in order to simulate the re-loading
of this module.

functions.name: haystack.utils.geo.ensure_geometry
documentation: Makes sure the parameter passed in looks like a GEOS `GEOSGeometry`.

functions.name: haystack.utils.geo.ensure_point
documentation: Makes sure the parameter passed in looks like a GEOS `Point`.

functions.name: haystack.utils.geo.ensure_wgs84
documentation:
Ensures the point passed in is a GEOS `Point` & returns that point's
data is in the WGS-84 spatial reference.

functions.name: haystack.utils.geo.ensure_distance
documentation: Makes sure the parameter passed in is a 'Distance' object.

functions.name: haystack.utils.geo.generate_bounding_box
documentation:
Takes two opposite corners of a bounding box (order matters!) & generates
a two-tuple of the correct coordinates for the bounding box.

The two-tuple is in the form `((min_lat, min_lng), (max_lat, max_lng))`.

class_variables.name: haystack.management.commands.update_index.Command.help
documentation: noqa A003

functions.name: haystack.utils.loading.load_backend
documentation:
Loads a backend for interacting with the search engine.

Requires a `backend_path`. It should be a string resembling a Python
import path, pointing to a `BaseEngine` subclass. The built-in options
available include:

```
* haystack.backends.solr.SolrEngine
* haystack.backends.xapian.XapianEngine (third-party)
* haystack.backends.whoosh.WhooshEngine
* haystack.backends.simple.SimpleEngine
```

If you've implemented a custom backend, you can provide the path to
your backend & matching `Engine` class. For example:

```
``myapp.search_backends.CustomSolrEngine``
```
Load the search backend.

functions.name: haystack.utils.loading.load_router
documentation:
Loads a router for choosing which connection to use.

Requires a `full_router_path`. It should be a string resembling a Python
import path, pointing to a `BaseRouter` subclass. The built-in options
available include:

```
* haystack.routers.DefaultRouter
```

If you've implemented a custom backend, you can provide the path to
your backend & matching `Engine` class. For example:

```
``myapp.search_routers.MasterSlaveRouter``
```

methods.name: haystack.utils.loading.UnifiedIndex.__init__
documentation: Used to collect all the indexes into a cohesive whole.
